findings.title: Transfer-in accounting trusts nominal amounts and ignores unsuccessful ERC20 return values

best_agent_match.title: Transfer-in accounting trusts the requested amount instead of the tokens actually received

findings.title: depositExactAmountETHMint skips WETH pool synchronization and can mint shares at a stale price

best_agent_match.title: depositExactAmountETHMint skips pool synchronization and can mint WETH shares at a stale price

findings.title: Verified isolation pools can bypass liquidation repayment invariants

best_agent_match.title: Pure collateral can be liquidated without health check

findings.title: Illiquid liquidation credits residual shares to the liquidator but records the token under the victim NFT

best_agent_match.title: Illiquid liquidation credits shares to the liquidator but records the token under the victim NFT

findings.title: Arbitrary NFT dusting combines with a uint8 loop to freeze position cleanup once enough markets are listed

best_agent_match.title: Anyone can dust arbitrary NFTs, and token-removal logic breaks once a position tracks more than 255 tokens

rejected_candidates.title: The per-pool allowBorrow flag is stored but never enforced in the audited borrow path
rejected_candidates.reason: Borrowing routes through `WISE_SECURITY.checksBorrow()`, and the security-module implementation is not present in scope. The available code is insufficient to prove that `allowBorrow` is unenforced.

rejected_candidates.title: Reentrancy vulnerability in liquidation due to unsafe external calls
rejected_candidates.reason: The report does not show a concrete reentrant profit path. ERC20 calls are external, but the claim relies on a malicious listed token and does not demonstrate an exploitable invariant break in the audited code.

rejected_candidates.title: Flash loan price manipulation vulnerability in liquidation
rejected_candidates.reason: The source only exposes an oracle interface and gives no evidence that liquidation uses flash-loan-manipulable spot pricing. The Chainlink manipulation claim is unsupported by the audited code.

rejected_candidates.title: Division by zero in share calculation functions
rejected_candidates.reason: Pools initialize `pseudoTotalPool`, `pseudoTotalBorrowAmount`, `totalDepositShares`, and `totalBorrowShares` to 1, so the cited denominators are not zero in normal empty-pool states.

rejected_candidates.title: Fee calculation can result in zero shares causing fee loss
rejected_candidates.reason: This is a protocol-fee rounding edge case, not a realistic user-loss, solvency, or theft issue.

rejected_candidates.title: Lack of Oracle dead switch - price manipulation via dead oracle
rejected_candidates.reason: Borrow and withdraw checks are delegated to external `WISE_SECURITY` hooks that are not included in scope, so the code shown does not prove a dead-oracle bypass.

rejected_candidates.title: Missing access control allows unauthorized position operations
rejected_candidates.reason: `approve()` only updates `allowance[msg.sender][poolToken][spender]`; it cannot set allowances for another user's NFT or position.

rejected_candidates.title: Inconsistent position lock checks across functions
rejected_candidates.reason: Potentially plausible, but `unCollateralizeDeposit()` immediately calls `WISE_SECURITY.checkUncollateralizedDeposit()`, and the security-module implementation is missing, so a real bypass is not established.

rejected_candidates.title: Race condition in _reduceAllowance allows double spending of allowance
rejected_candidates.reason: Allowance checks and decrements occur atomically within each call, and no concurrent or reentrant double-spend path is demonstrated.

rejected_candidates.title: No slippage protection in liquidation
rejected_candidates.reason: This is generic MEV/front-running exposure for liquidators, not a protocol-level vulnerability affecting pool safety.

rejected_candidates.title: LASA algorithm can be manipulated via deposit/withdraw timing
rejected_candidates.reason: The claim is speculative and does not show a concrete path to extract value, steal funds, or break solvency.

rejected_candidates.title: Immutable WISE_SECURITY after initial setup - no upgrade path
rejected_candidates.reason: A one-time security-module configuration is a governance/design choice, not a standalone vulnerability.

rejected_candidates.title: Timestamp dependence in LASA algorithm
rejected_candidates.reason: Minor timestamp influence on a three-hour control loop is too weak and speculative to be reportable.

rejected_candidates.title: Pure collateral can be liquidated without health check
rejected_candidates.reason: The code intentionally includes pure collateral in liquidation recovery, and the report does not show a separate health-check bypass beyond that designed behavior.
